title: pyosys: __getitem__ for supported classes
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

This line no longer worked: https://github.com/donn/nl2bench/blob/e1fcf29dab450dce6e87a84d5d3466f1ae296a6e/nl2bench/nl_parser.py#L142

745222fa3bf2ac570935ffd044c86fa2eb12d123 made it so the result of `.bits()` was not iterable in Python.

Furthermore, after fixing it, this line failed:

https://github.com/donn/nl2bench/blob/e1fcf29dab450dce6e87a84d5d3466f1ae296a6e/nl2bench/nl_parser.py#L87

This was I think a regression by myself.

_Explain how this is achieved._

- functions that have a const `[]` operator method now support `__getitem__` in Python
- fields of a pointer type now return a `reference_internal` instead of a `copy` because classes referenced to by pointers typically aren't copyable (e.g. RTLIL::Wire, RTLIL::Module, etc)
- removed duplicate pyosys test

_Make sure your change comes with tests. If not possible, share how a reviewer might evaluate it._

A new test has been added. I also updated an existing test that is now failing…? Not sure who's maintaining the .ys version of this test though.
